surface.name: Deploy and open a sample Cloud Run container
surface.selection_basis: Workshop selection policy (2026-07-19): when official docs present peer routes, commit to the fastest and most commonly used documented route. Prefer the vendor's first-listed or recommended quickstart, dashboard/console over CLI when both are peers, and the route with an explicit vendor time claim or fewest documented steps when tied. Selected route: Deploy and open a sample Cloud Run container. The general Google Cloud router does not establish one product outcome. Its application-development path links product quickstarts, and the Cloud Run sample-container quickstart provides a complete console route from project and billing prerequisites through a running public URL. Prior surface before policy commit: Google Cloud documentation get-started router.

alternatives_considered.name: Application development setup
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: It is one of six co-equal job-function paths on the platform get-started page.

alternatives_considered.name: Cloud APIs getting started
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: It is a cross-product API-access route, not a vendor-selected default for all Google Cloud developers.

alternatives_considered.name: Google Cloud CLI installation quickstart
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: Installing and initializing the CLI is a tooling route and not a platform workload success.

alternatives_considered.name: Cloud Run container deployment quickstart
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: It is a Cloud Run product-specific deployment route among many Google Cloud products.

entry_point.developer_intent: Start using Google Cloud after choosing the platform but before choosing a product or job-function journey.
assumed_prior_state: Discovery is complete and the developer has selected Google Cloud.
No Google Cloud product, workload, interface, or job-function path has been selected.

documented_first_success.official_milestone: The Cloud Run sample-container quickstart ends when the deployed service's public URL returns the sample container's response in the browser.
documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: Create a project with billing, deploy the Cloud Run sample container, and open its public service URL to see the container's response.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: The browser displays the response served by the deployed Cloud Run sample container at its service URL.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: The browser displays the response from the deployed Cloud Run sample container.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: Selected under workshop fastest/most-used policy; terminal taken from the committed official Cloud Run sample-container candidate route.

prerequisites.requirement: A Google Cloud account is the stated starting requirement; the page offers account creation and free credits.
A job function or product workload must be selected to determine the relevant setup sequence.

primary_path.action: Open Get started with Google Cloud.
details: The page first offers account creation or console access.
It then presents platform setup by job function.
primary_path.success_signal: The account actions and job-function options are visible.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: No wait is documented at the router.

primary_path.action: Sign in to a Google Cloud account or create one if new to Google Cloud.
details: New customers are offered $300 in Welcome credit for 90 days through the Free Trial.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Account identity, organization policy, or login recovery can block console access.

primary_path.action: If using the optional Free Trial route for billing, start the trial, provide the requested name, address, and payment method for identity verification, and complete any work-account one-time-passcode email challenge.
details: The Cloud Run quickstart requires a billing-enabled project, but it does not require Free Trial enrollment. A developer with an existing active billing account skips this step.
The payment method is used for identity verification; covered trial usage is not billed unless the developer later upgrades to pay as you go.
primary_path.input: Billing identity and supported payment method
primary_path.output: Free Trial billing account with $300 Welcome credit
primary_path.success_signal: For the optional Free Trial route, the billing account is active and the Welcome credit is available.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Payment validation can require another supported method or a retry after 24 hours. A pending authorization can remain for 1 to 14 business days depending on the bank.

primary_path.action: On the project selector page, select an existing project or create a new Google Cloud project.
details: Creating a project requires roles/resourcemanager.projectCreator and resourcemanager.projects.create; selecting a project requires only an existing granted role.
primary_path.input: Project name or existing project access
primary_path.success_signal: The intended project is active in the console selector.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Without Project Creator, ask an administrator to create the project or grant the required role.

primary_path.action: Verify that billing is enabled for the selected project.
details: The quickstart requires billing even when Free Trial credit is used.
primary_path.input: Selected project and active billing account
primary_path.success_signal: The project is linked to an active billing account.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Link an authorized billing account or obtain billing permission before Cloud Run deployment.

primary_path.action: Verify the developer has Cloud Run Admin on the service, Service Account User on the service identity, and Logs Viewer on the project.
primary_path.success_signal: All three required roles are granted directly or through equivalent custom or predefined roles.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Ask an administrator to grant missing roles before attempting deployment.

primary_path.action: Open the Cloud Run page in the Google Cloud console.
primary_path.success_signal: The Cloud Run navigation and Services view are available.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Wrong project selection, disabled access, or missing permission must be corrected.

primary_path.action: Select Services, then select Deploy container.
primary_path.success_signal: The deployment-source and service settings are visible.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Missing Cloud Run permission can prevent service creation.

primary_path.action: Ensure Deploy one revision from an existing container image is selected.
primary_path.success_signal: The Container image URL control is enabled.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Selecting source or continuous deployment creates a different journey.

primary_path.action: In Container image URL, select Test with a sample container.
primary_path.success_signal: The sample image URL populates the field.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A custom image requires its own registry, build, and access path and is outside this candidate.

primary_path.action: Enter a service name or keep the default.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Invalid or conflicting names must be corrected.

primary_path.action: Keep the default europe-west1 region or select the intended Cloud Run region.
details: Cloud Run is regional; the guide says latency, availability, durability, colocated products, and cost can affect the choice.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Organization constraints or unavailable service features can require another allowed region.

primary_path.action: In Authentication, select Allow public access.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A domain restriction organization policy can forbid unauthenticated invocation; the developer must use the documented private-service testing route instead.

primary_path.action: Select Create and wait for Cloud Run to deploy the container.
primary_path.success_signal: Deployment completes and a URL appears beside URL:.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Build, image, billing, IAM, quota, organization-policy, or regional errors must be corrected and deployment retried.

primary_path.action: Copy the displayed service URL, paste it into a browser, and open it.
primary_path.success_signal: The browser displays the response from the deployed Cloud Run sample container.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: If public access is restricted, authenticate and test the private service using the linked private-service instructions; otherwise inspect deployment and service logs.

branches.condition: The intended role is IT administration.
branches.path: The platform page orders three setup actions: establish the organization, administrators, and billing; create an initial architecture; then deploy or download the configuration. Quotas and deployment-archetype resources are labeled for later.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Produces an organization and architecture setup journey with administrative and billing gates, not an application execution journey.

branches.condition: The intended role is FinOps administration.
branches.path: The platform page orders four setup actions: learn about Cloud Billing, create a billing account, set up spending notifications, and enable billing-data export for custom reporting. Billing reports, FinOps Hub, resource-hierarchy, and cost-optimization resources are labeled for later.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Produces a billing and cost-observability journey whose account, permissions, and terminal state differ from developer setup.

branches.condition: The intended role is security engineering.
branches.path: The platform page orders five setup actions: set up API access, install the gcloud CLI, learn IAM, understand identity management, and learn organization policies. Supply-chain security, Security Command Center, and Well-Architected security resources are labeled for later.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Produces a security and policy orientation journey rather than one executed workload or universal completion signal.

branches.condition: The intended role is DevOps engineering.
branches.path: The platform page orders six setup actions: set up API access, install the gcloud CLI, enable teammates with IAM, install a Cloud Client Library, learn authentication, and learn authorization and access control. Observability, infrastructure as code, and a container CI/CD architecture are labeled for later.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Produces a collaboration and tooling setup journey distinct from application development and does not establish a universal executed-workload boundary on this page.

branches.condition: The intended role is application development.
branches.path: The platform page orders six setup actions: set up API access, install the gcloud CLI, install a Cloud Client Library, set up IDE extensions, learn authentication, and learn authorization and access control. A generative-AI model and infrastructure choice is labeled for later, and the route does not end in an executed workload on this page.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Requires a later product or API choice to establish an observable application result.

branches.condition: The intended role is data analysis.
branches.path: The platform page orders four setup actions: load and query sample data, explore, analyze, and share data, learn programmatic analysis tools, and complete an introduction to AI and ML in BigQuery. bq CLI, Gemini in BigQuery, and analytics design-pattern resources are labeled for later.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Produces a data-analysis journey distinct from application development or infrastructure setup.

branches.condition: The intended outcome is a deployed application.
branches.path: A product-specific route such as the Cloud Run container deployment quickstart must be selected.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Changes the path from general platform setup to a concrete product deployment journey.

branches.condition: An eligible new user chooses the Google Cloud Free Trial.
branches.path: Start Free Trial signup; provide a name, address, and payment method for identity verification; if using a work account, receive the emailed invitation and enter its one-time passcode to prove ownership; resolve any payment-method validation error or supported-payment-method issue and retry, waiting 24 hours before another attempt if the error persists; allow a pending payment authorization to remain for 1 to 14 business days depending on the bank; receive a Free Trial billing account preloaded with $300 of Welcome credit valid for 90 days; use covered products without being billed during the trial; manually select Upgrade only if pay-as-you-go billing is wanted. If the credit is exhausted or 90 days pass without upgrade, the trial billing account auto-closes and workloads shut down. Upgrade within the following 30 days to reinstate service and recover trial resources and data, or allow the resources to be deleted after that grace period.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds conditional identity, payment-method, authorization-wait, email-passcode, error-retry, credit, shutdown, and recovery states. It is not a universal Google Cloud prerequisite and billing starts only after manual upgrade.

branches.condition: The developer wants limited BigQuery exploration without starting the Free Trial.
branches.path: Enable the BigQuery sandbox and use its limited BigQuery capabilities without providing a credit card or creating a billing account for the project.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Removes the Free Trial payment-method gate but narrows the journey to BigQuery sandbox capabilities rather than a platform-wide Google Cloud path.

friction_gates.description: The official platform get-started page begins with creating a Google Cloud account or going to the console.
A job function, product, or workload must be selected before the journey and terminal success can be defined.

The optional Free Trial asks for name, address, and a supported payment method for identity verification. A pending authorization can remain for 1 to 14 business days, but Google states it is not a charge and does not bill unless the user manually upgrades.

Free Trial signup can require a work-account email one-time passcode, payment-method troubleshooting, a 24-hour retry wait after persistent signup errors, and a 1 to 14 business-day pending authorization.

sources.title: Get started with Google Cloud
evidence_supported: Absence of one platform-level terminal state

sources.title: Quickstart: Install the Google Cloud CLI
Quickstart: Deploy a container to Cloud Run
evidence_supported: Separate Cloud Run product deployment route

sources.title: Google Cloud Free Trial FAQs
sections_used: What's included in the Google Cloud Free Trial?
Why is payment information required for a free trial?
Will I be billed after the trial ends?
How do I cancel my trial?
Why am I getting an error during signup?
Can I still explore Google Cloud even if I'm not ready to signup for the Google Cloud Free Trial?
Can I sign into Google Cloud with my work account?
evidence_supported: Name, address, and payment-method identity verification
Signup error, retry, and email-passcode paths

uncertainties.question: Which Google Cloud job function or product should define the canonical workshop journey?
uncertainties.impact: The answer changes the prerequisites, billing and permission gates, tools, execution steps, and success signal.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The official platform page presents six job-function routes and many product-specific paths without selecting a universal default.

excluded_after_success: Any product-specific account, billing, project, API, CLI, deployment, or data-analysis steps until a human selects the bounded Google Cloud route
